_comment: Shared, platform-neutral smoke-test scenario manifest. Consumed by BOTH run_smoke.ps1 (Windows) and run_smoke.sh (Linux/macOS). Each runner maps the logical binary names (IometerQt, dynamotest, Dynamo, Iometertest, IOmeter) to real per-OS paths; everything else here is shared. 'modes': test=dynamotest/synthetic non-elevated, real=production Dynamo (real disk, needs elevation). 'kind' selects the runner behavior. 'status':'pending' scenarios are skipped unless -IncludePending/--include-pending.

scenarios.name: icf-validate
scenarios.description: Shipped app parses every fixture ICF through the shared core (no Dynamo).

scenarios.name: qt-demo
scenarios.description: Qt demo engine starts, runs, and exits cleanly (no Dynamo).

scenarios.name: qt-dynamotest
scenarios.description: Qt GUI (batch) + dynamotest synthetic worker. Non-elevated.

scenarios.name: mfc-dynamotest
scenarios.description: MFC GUI (asInvoker) + a non-local-manager ICF (TESTMGR @ localhost) so the GUI spawns nothing, with dynamotest launched separately as the TESTMGR worker. Fully non-elevated, no UAC. The '-m localhost' is required so IOmeter can call back to the worker (a placeholder name would hang); the manager-restore fix in ManagerList.cpp (single non-local manager -> wait, don't auto-spawn local) is what makes this work.

scenarios.name: qt-real
scenarios.description: Qt GUI (batch) + REAL Dynamo on a real disk (elevated). KNOWN ISSUE: Qt real-disk path errors on every I/O (0 IOps / high error count). See README.

scenarios.name: mfc-real
scenarios.description: MFC GUI (asInvoker) auto-spawns the REAL Dynamo ELEVATED via ShellExecuteEx(runas) for raw-disk access; the GUI itself stays unelevated. Known-good reference path.
